calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (in person):

GREGORY S. WALSTON, Esq., THE WALSTON LAW GROUP, (415) 956-9200, for Plaintiff.

G. SCOTT EMBLIDGE, Esq., MOSCONE EMBLIDGE & RUBENS LLP, (415) 362-3599, for Defendants.

RE: Defendant's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

Ruling is Argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court takes the matter under submission.

Counsel for prevailing party is to submit a proposed order as to form via email to Department 302.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-24-2023 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-23-2023 Continued To Mar-22-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prove Up Hearinghearing
Off Calendar Feb-01-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:04:23 a.m., pausing at 10:28:55 a.m., reconvening at 10:32:53 a.m. and concluding at 10:36:42 a.m.

Plaintiff Walston and Associates, Inc. DBA The Walston Law Group represented by Gregory S. Walston, Esq.
Defendant Nicole Ashar not appearing.

GREGORY S. WALSTON is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Court has reviewed the case and all filings pertaining to this hearing.
Counsel for Plaintiff presents outline and summary of case.

Court examines GREGORY S. WALSTON re: retainer agreement, invoicing, billing software, and Plaintiff's exhibits.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit B - All invoices totaling $17,802.86
Plaintiff's Exhibit C - Billed Time for Walton & Associates vs. Ashar

GREGORY S. WALSTON is further examined re: attorney fees, costs, and calculation of interest.

The matter is submitted.
Based on the summary of the case, comments made by Plaintiff's Counsel, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has proven each essential element of the complaint and Defendant is in breach of contract/retainer agreement.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Walston and Associates, Inc. DBA The Walston Law Group, and against Nicole Ashar, as follows:

Court awards interest of 7% from Feb-15-2021 to Feb-01-223.
Plaintiff's request for attorney fees is DENIED.

1. Damages: $17,802.86
2. Interest: $2,441.56
3. Costs: $1,530.65

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $21,775.07

Plaintiff's request for judgment against Joseph Petroziello is DENIED Without Prejudice.
Proposed judgment signed as modified, filed, and served in open court.
